name: KnowBe4
description: Security awareness training and simulated phishing platform (KMSAT). Yields evidence of training campaign completion, phishing simulation click and report rates, and organisational and per-user risk scores.

steps.action: Sign in to the KSAT console and navigate to the Reports tab
steps.permissions_required: Full Admin, or a security role with Reports permission
steps.security_note: Use a named admin account with the least privilege needed; report viewing does not require Full Admin

steps.action: Generate a phishing report (e.g. Phishing Security Test Results) and set the date range to cover the collection period
steps.audit_note: Ensure the date range abuts the previous collection period so there are no coverage gaps

steps.action: Generate a training report (e.g. Training Campaign Enrollments) showing completion status for active campaigns over the same period
steps.audit_note: Completion percentages against total enrolled users demonstrate training coverage, not just activity

steps.action: From the Dashboard, capture a screenshot of the organisational Risk Score and Phish-Prone Percentage with the console URL and date visible
steps.audit_note: Risk Score trend over time is stronger evidence than a single point-in-time value

steps.action: Download the generated reports as CSV or PDF from the Download Center and save with date-stamped filenames
steps.audit_note: Generated reports remain in the Download Center for 90 days only — download before expiry

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Reports contain employee names, email addresses and failure data — handle as personal data under your data protection policy

steps.action: Navigate to the Reports tab, open the Scheduled Reports subtab and click Schedule Report
steps.permissions_required: Full Admin, or a security role with Reports permission

steps.action: Select the report types needed as evidence — phishing security test results (click/report rates) and training campaign completion — and set the recurrence to match your evidence collection cadence (e.g. monthly or quarterly)
steps.audit_note: Align the report period with the schedule so consecutive reports form a continuous record

steps.action: Add report recipients; each recipient receives a notification email with a link to download the report in PDF or CSV format
steps.security_note: Limit recipients to authorised compliance personnel — reports contain per-user failure data

steps.action: On each delivery, download the report via the emailed link and file it in the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.audit_note: Reports carry a KnowBe4 generation timestamp; download links and Download Center copies expire after 90 days, so file promptly

steps.action: Review the Scheduled Reports subtab quarterly to confirm schedules are still active and cover all live phishing and training campaigns
steps.audit_note: A schedule silently disabled or scoped to an ended campaign creates an evidence gap

steps.action: In the KSAT console, click your email address (top right) > Account Settings > Account Integrations > API, enable Reporting API Access and click Create New API Token
steps.security_note: The token is displayed once only — store it immediately in a secrets manager. Only the last 10 digits are visible afterwards; if lost, create a new token.

steps.action: Configure a script or integration to call GET /v1/phishing/security_tests and GET /v1/phishing/security_tests/{pst_id}/recipients on your regional base URL (us.api.knowbe4.com, eu.api.knowbe4.com, ca.api.knowbe4.com, uk.api.knowbe4.com or de.api.knowbe4.com) with an Authorization: Bearer header, capturing delivered/opened/clicked/reported counts
Also collect GET /v1/training/enrollments for training completion status and GET /v1/users for per-user Phish-Prone Percentage and current risk scores; paginate with page and per_page parameters
steps.audit_note: Enrollment records include status and completion dates, giving a per-user audit trail

steps.action: Schedule the collection via cron or a CI/CD pipeline to run weekly, writing timestamped JSON exports to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Store the API token in a secrets manager, never in plaintext configuration, and rotate it on your credential rotation schedule

steps.action: Implement error handling with alerting on failed runs, and back off on HTTP 429 responses to stay within KnowBe4's published rate limits
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within your SLA and be recorded

steps.action: Validate each run against expected volumes — user count versus active headcount and enrolment count versus campaign size — and flag anomalies
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: KnowBe4 Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: KMSAT does not provide webhooks or native event streaming; the closest real alternative is continuous high-frequency polling of the Reporting API, either directly or via a SIEM cloud connector (e.g. Splunk, Sumo Logic or Microsoft Sentinel integrations built on the same API)
steps.audit_note: Document the polling interval as the collection latency bound — there is no push mechanism to cite

steps.action: Deploy the pipeline with a dedicated Reporting API token created solely for this integration, polling users, phishing security test results and training enrolments on a fixed schedule
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated token per integration so it can be revoked independently; store it in a secrets manager

steps.action: Configure completeness metrics: enrolled users versus active headcount, phishing campaign coverage percentage, records collected per run and collection latency
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Set up automated quality checks — schema validation of API responses, duplicate suppression keyed on record IDs, and gap detection between consecutive polling windows
Create a dashboard showing collection health (runs per day, failure rate, coverage %) alongside the organisational Risk Score and Phish-Prone Percentage trend
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of both process health and awareness programme effectiveness

steps.action: Implement an automated quarterly review that generates a collection health report and flags SLA breaches or campaigns missing from the evidence set
